description: The dead-ideas registry — every strategy idea this project has tested, with its epitaph. The foundry reads this before every round: new ideas must not descend from dead lineages, and must engage the failure lessons. Deaths are data (philosophy.md: a losing strategy that produces clear answers beats a winning one that doesn't).
failure_lessons: FEE FLOOR: ~0.6% round-trip on $200 positions. Edges under ~1%/trade are structurally dead regardless of hit rate. Confirmed twice: textbook sweep (gross +0.03%/trade, net -0.18%) and weekend_illiquidity_momentum (gross-positive, 49.4% win rate, fee-negative).
CONSTRAINT DOMINANCE: 5 slots, $1k ceiling, 1h/symbol cooldown kill 99.6% of frequent signals. Fitness = edge per constraint slot. Frequency is worthless; amplitude per fire is everything.
FIXED EXITS: +5% target / -3% stop / 24h time exit, checked every 5min. Ideas needing custom exits are inadmissible; ideas whose wins resolve slower than 24h are silently truncated.

CANON LINEAGES DIE: both indicator mean-reversion AND the surviving retail structural plays (documented lineages: capitulation reversal, lead-lag, ORB, volume footprint, weekend effect) tested negative net of fees. The arbitraged-away frontier is past the documented-retail-edge line.
GROSS-POSITIVE IS THE TRAP SHAPE: several ideas found real but too-small structure. The question for a new idea is not 'is there signal' but 'why would the remaining signal exceed 1% per event'.

FADE-SHAPED ENTRIES DIE (r001): every entry that fades a move (wick rejection, overshoot snap, band reversion) has lost against the +5%/-3%/24h exit, which structurally rewards follow-through. Propose continuation/initiation shapes, or argue explicitly why a fade survives an asymmetric exit.

THE GATE SURVIVED, THE ENGINE DIED (r001 exploit direction): drawdown_regime_contrarian_gate scored the best numbers ever tested (43.3% win, gross Sharpe 0.98, -0.33% net) — the self-referential regime gate (null-arm win rate + stop-out cluster via context_keys=['system_state']) carries real information, but the 24-bar breakout engine behind it is too weak. Strong prior: pair BETTER ENGINES with the same meta-gate.
CALIBRATE FIRE RATES (r001): predictions missed by 3x-8x in both directions. State the base-rate arithmetic (how many qualifying events per 288-bar day) rather than intuiting rarity.

HORIZON IS A LEVER (2026-07-17): per-variant tp/sl/time_exit_hours are now honored in both replay and live. The fee floor is fixed per trade, so theses resolving over days targeting 8-20% moves face ~10x better cost ratios than 24h theses targeting 1-2%.

COST IS A DESIGN INPUT (2026-07-17): prefer entries where price comes to the level (limit-friendly) and single-fire selectivity — the denominator of edge-per-slot includes what execution costs, not just what the signal knows.

CONJUNCTIONS MULTIPLY TO ZERO (r002): base-rate arithmetic that multiplies per-condition probabilities as if independent produces fire-rate misses of 4x to infinity when the conjuncts are anti-correlated (high volume x small body is nearly mutually exclusive in crypto candles; entropy jumps of +0.35 bits are impossible when H already sits at 0.95/1.0). Every spec must state WHY its conditions are independent, and any idea whose premise can be checked against the data distribution BEFORE a full gauntlet (entropy ceiling, conjunct correlation) should be — a 5-minute histogram would have killed two of five r002 ideas.

NAMED-TIME COILS DIE (r001-r003, 4 deaths): dead_zone (overnight), options_expiry_pin (08:00 UTC), weekend thinness, asian_london handoff (07:00-09:00). A range break carries no extra directional energy for happening at a named clock time — the calendar lens is 0-for-4 on release-shaped ideas and needs a fundamentally different mechanism to earn another slot.

THE GATE KEEPS WINNING, THE ENGINES KEEP STARVING (r001-r003): the self-referential system-state gate is best-of-round three rounds running on three different engines, and r003's version is the first idea ever to pass its own kill criterion (+1.75%/trade, but n=7). The binding constraint is now ENGINE FIRE RATE: a gated idea needs an ungated engine firing >=1/sym/day for the armed sample to reach statistical decidability inside 930d.

THE GATE ITSELF IS THE BOTTLENECK, NOT ENGINE FIRE RATE (r004 refutes the r001-r003 fix): trend_persistence_regime_gated_engine paired the gate with a momentum-persistence engine that comfortably cleared the prescribed >=1/sym/day (5.17/sym/day raw at premise check) — and the armed sample still collapsed to n=39 over 930d (0.008/sym/day, ~27x under prediction), the worst win rate of any gate-family idea yet (25.6%). Four rounds, four engines, and NO gated idea has ever reached n>=100 armed trades (r001 n=460, r002 n=109/35, r003 n=7, r004 n=39). The bottleneck was never the engine's raw frequency — it is the gate's own conjunctive definition (multiple system_state conditions holding simultaneously) being intrinsically rare. A looser or single-condition gate is required before the family can be tested again.

MICROSTRUCTURE-FROM-OHLCV LENS EXHAUSTED (r001-r004, 4-for-4 dead): wick rejection (r001, fade-shaped), absorption (r002, empty conjunction), gap-fill exhaustion (r003, starved 2000x under prediction), one-sided range expansion (r004, well-sampled n=465 and still net -0.71%/trade, worse than a random large bar). Every candle-geometry shape tried — fade, absorption, gap, one-sided thrust — has failed. The lens needs order-book/footprint data to proceed; OHLCV candle geometry alone is exhausted.

BEHAVIORAL-CALENDAR LENS EXHAUSTED IN BOTH DIRECTIONS (r001-r004, 4-for-4 dead): the round-number price level was tested as a fade (r001, overshoot-snap) and as its deliberate inversion, a stop-cascade continuation (r004, breach-continuation) — both dead, both under/over-fired their base rate by an order of magnitude. Named clock-time coils were tested twice more (options-expiry r002, Asian-London r003) — also dead. No remaining calendar/price-level permutation is untested; the lens is closed absent a genuinely new data source (real order-book or open-interest, not an OHLCV-derived time or price artifact).

THE CEILING IS MEASURED (2026-07-18, instrument-exemption ML study): walk-forward gradient boosting with full feature-engineering hindsight tops out at gross +0.02% to +0.2%/trade for horizons of 1h-12h — the sub-12h OHLCV search space is EMPTY below the 0.6% cost floor, and sub-12h theses are inadmissible unless their entire mechanism is cost engineering. At 24h the ceiling reaches gross ~+0.6% (net ~breakeven taker, ~+0.25% maker): the only admissible OHLCV territory is 24h+ horizons with maker-friendly entries — or leaving OHLCV for the Layer-2 context data now being recorded.

LITERATURE PRIORS (2026-07-18, external survey): no published crypto effect clears US-retail-spot costs at hours-to-days horizons — converging with the measured ceiling from outside. Slippage is VOLATILITY-CONDITIONAL (worst when event signals fire; flat slippage assumptions flatter event strategies). Funding signals are crash-risk premia, not free alpha (BIS); directional funding-extreme rules are untested folklore. The open bands: maker execution at 24h, and 1-8 WEEK holds (published persistence; amortizes the floor; unmeasurable in our archive - the live/shadow channel is the instrument there).

SHOCKS MEAN-REVERT AT MULTI-DAY HORIZON (2026-07-19, measured control arm): sign-aligned forward returns after 3%/3x-vol shock bars are -3.0% (24h), -4.3% (72h), -3.3% (120h), n=53 full population — impulse-chasing entries (r002 R0, r003 post_shock, r004 hawkes lineage) lost not because signals were absent but because the true multi-day signal is CONTRARIAN. A multi-day shock-FADE spec is admissible; it must survive the standard premortem+gauntlet and respect the volatility-conditional slippage prior (costs spike exactly at these events). Retest adverse selection is CONFIRMED both-arms (+0.98% never-retested vs -0.89% retested): maker-band entries must come from populations where waiting is not adverse selection.

GATE FAMILY FROZEN (2026-07-19, hierarchical meta-analysis): pooling all four gate variants (random-effects, winner's-curse-biased UPWARD) gives family mean -0.62%/trade +-0.21 and P(family effect > 0) of 0.05-0.5% under skeptical-to-sympathetic priors — the r003 n=7 pass scores BELOW the expected best-of-5 under pure noise (z +1.5 vs +1.8). NO new self-referential-gate spec may be synthesized until the cross-engine transfer test is run and positive: freeze the r003 gate rule, apply to every existing engine's trade stream, median gated-minus-ungated delta must exceed zero, with a shuffled-sequence placebo showing null. A family surviving on the memory of its last small-sample outlier is the regression-to-the-mean trap; this freeze is its pre-registered stop. AMENDMENT (2026-07-20, round 005): the freeze protocol was not followed — round 005 synthesized two more new self-referential-gate specs (placebo_losing_streak_single_gate_trend, placebo_streak_gated_weekly_trend_engine) without running the prescribed cross-engine transfer test. Despite that, both died, and placebo_streak_gated_weekly_trend_engine reached the largest gate-family sample yet after r001 (n=407/447) with its gated win rate statistically indistinguishable from its own round's ungated engine (30.7%/30.0% vs 29.5%/29.5%) — satisfying its own pre-registered retirement clause. The family is now CLOSED, not frozen: five rounds, five gate specs, one n=7 outlier that regressed to the pooled mean exactly as predicted, zero times beating an ungated engine at a decidable sample. No future round may synthesize a self-referential-gate spec without a specific, named new mechanism for HOW the gate would carry information an ungated engine's own trade stream does not already contain.

SLOW-BAND FIRST MEASUREMENT (2026-07-19, selection window only): naive 4-week momentum with 2-week holds nets +0.49%/trade at n=120 — the first strategy-shaped positive at n>=100 in project history — and its matched drift null (unconditional weekly buys, same exits, n=287) nets -0.04% (gross +0.46% ~= the fee floor). The momentum increment over drift is +0.5%/trade but sub-1-SE: a lead, not an edge. The slow band is BACKTESTABLE (per-variant exits made it so) and now has a measured baseline: any 2-8-week spec must beat the DRIFT NULL, not zero — unconditional crypto buying covered costs in this window, so absolute positives at slow horizons are the gross-positive trap wearing a longer coat.

REVERSION AT EVERY MEASURED HORIZON (2026-07-20, operator-designed long-horizon probe, selection window only, ALL cells reported): long-or-flat momentum underperformed unconditional drift in 9/9 cells of the {1,3,6}mo-lookback x {1,3,6}mo-hold grid (increments -2.5% to -16.4%); high-funding (crowded) months ran +1.8%/30d vs +3.4% for low-funding (direction matches BIS crash-premium and the measured multi-day shock-fade). Unified read: 2024-25 crypto in this basket REVERTS at multi-day through monthly scales while drift dominates long-only returns — continuation was tested in many costumes under continuation-rewarding exits and died everywhere; the live structure is reversion + drift. Caveats: one regime, ~2 effective assets, n=13-14 at 6mo cells; the 2-week TSMOM result (momentum BEAT drift) marks the weekly-to-monthly crossover as open. Specs engaging reversion must still beat the DRIFT null, and asymmetric exits must match the thesis shape (a reversion thesis under pro-continuation exits is self-sabotage — see FADE-SHAPED and the exit-confound test). AMENDMENTS (same day, external referee): (1) REGIME CAVEAT — the layered-horizon literature (short reversal / medium continuation / long reversal) says horizon structure is regime-dependent, and crypto trends violently in bull phases; 'reverts everywhere' may be a fact about 2024-26, not about crypto. Lesson 22 is PROVISIONAL until the same grid is run on 2017-2023 daily bars incl. the 2020-21 trend regime (in progress). (2) CANARY MANDATE — every future round must include at least ONE continuation-shaped spec regardless of the prevailing worldview, so a regime flip announces itself in our own results rather than in a drawdown. A unifying theory inherited by the generator is exactly where registry-feedback inbreeding accelerates under the banner of insight. (3) Reversion-inside-drift specs (pullback buying) are knife-catchers in a regime break: their kill criteria must reference the drift assumption explicitly.

REGIME IS THE VARIABLE (2026-07-20, regime-split grid on 2017-present dailies — supersedes the naive reading of the prior lesson): DIRECTIONAL (long/short, drift-free) monthly momentum earned +28.8% per hold in 2020-21, +21.6% in 2022, +13.4% in 2023 — then FLIPPED SIGN to -12.4% (0/9 cells) in 2024-25, which is our ENTIRE archive. Every verdict this project has produced — the ceiling, the graveyard, the reversion signature — is conditioned on one side of a coin that flips on multi-year scales. Consequences: (1) long-or-flat 'momentum' increments mostly measure drift-timing, not signal — use directional measurements; (2) any spec fit to the archive learns the reversion epoch and INVERTS if the regime flips back — the continuation canary is load-bearing, not decorative; (3) slow regime detection (e.g. trailing 12mo directional-momentum profitability) is a first-class research question, arguably THE question; (4) live/shadow evidence from the current epoch cannot validate behavior in the other one — epoch-humility in every promotion decision.

RARE-TOUCH CONJUNCTIONS MISS WORST OF ALL (r003 + r005): the two largest base-rate misses on record both required a PRICE TO TOUCH A SPECIFIC LEVEL WITHIN A WINDOW while a slower regime condition also held — slot_scarcity_conviction_gate (r003, n=7, ~166x under) and weekly_pullback_limit_into_uptrend (r005, n=3, ~186x under). Both cleared every arithmetic sanity check on paper (positively-correlated conjuncts, explicit base-rate math) and both still missed by two orders of magnitude, worse than any non-touch conjunction (4x-28x). A resting-limit-touch condition inside a slow regime is not like an OHLCV threshold conjunction — the touch almost never coincides with the regime being live over the sampled history. Premise-check actual touch frequency against the target regime (not just the regime's own frequency) before spending a gauntlet slot on any future 'price comes to a level, inside a multi-day regime' design.

PREMORTEM CANNOT SEE REGIME RISK (r005 multiday_magnitude_persistence_directional_hold, r006 multiweek_directional_regime_persistence_hold — 2nd occurrence, promoted): both ideas cleared premortem paper review with an explicit IMPLEMENT verdict ('no fatal flaw found') and both died anyway on the identical underlying mechanism (REGIME IS THE VARIABLE: directional monthly-scale momentum inverted to negative across this project's entire 2024-25 archive). Premortem's error classes — arithmetic miscalibration, closed-lens reattempt, regime conflict visible IN THE SPEC ITSELF, post-hoc sign-fitting — are all paper-detectable; a spec that correctly locates itself in genuinely open, previously-untested territory (as both of these did) is by construction invisible to a paper review whose only evidence base is the same measured archive the mechanism may be about to invert against. A clean premortem IMPLEMENT verdict means 'no flaw found on paper,' not 'likely to survive' — the CANARY MANDATE exists precisely because this class of failure can only be caught empirically, and both canary-shaped specs did their job: they failed informatively instead of silently.

KILL CRITERIA PROMISING A MATCHED CONTROL ARE NEVER ACTUALLY RUN (r006 constraint_rejection_pressure_release_engine, r007 cross_sectional_dispersion_gate_trend_engine — 2nd occurrence, promoted): both gate-family specs wrote kill_criteria requiring the gated engine to beat the SAME engine run ungated, framing that comparison as the methodological fix for the family's open reopening question — and in both rounds the gauntlet never registered or ran the ungated companion variant needed to evaluate it. The gauntlet script replays "each candidate alone" (per its own header); a kill_criterion that needs a matched control is not decided by that infrastructure unless the control is explicitly written up and registered as its own STRATEGY_VARIANTS entry and run in the same gauntlet pass. Both ideas died on their fallback net-expectancy clause instead, so absolute performance settled the verdict either way — but the deeper question (does external cross-sectional/synchronization information carry real lift over an engine's own signal) has now gone unanswered twice in a row. Any future spec whose kill_criterion depends on a matched control must register that control as a second, explicit STRATEGY_VARIANTS entry in the same round, or the comparison will not happen.

PREMISE-CHECK RAW COUNTS DO NOT PREDICT CONSTRAINED PLACEMENT (r007, 2 of 5 ideas this round): liquidity_window_shock_fade_maker's redesign raised its raw premise-check population from ~53 to 728 events (480 maker-fills) and epidemic_susceptible_depletion_terminal_burst's redesign raised its raw conjunction population from ~3 to 310 — both roughly two-orders-of-magnitude fixes aimed squarely at the sample-size objections premortem raised. Neither translated: the constrained gauntlet placed only 41 and 42 trades respectively, below the n>=100 floor both redesigns were meant to clear. CONSTRAINT DOMINANCE already names the mechanism (5 slots, $1k ceiling, 1h/symbol cooldown), but the specific new lesson is that a premise check run against the UNCONSTRAINED bar history is not a proxy for the constrained gauntlet's placed-trade count when the strategy's hold time (96h, 48h) occupies a symbol's slot for multiple days per fire — occupancy, not raw signal frequency, is the binding constraint for any multi-day-hold spec, and premise checks must model slot-occupancy under the actual constraint schedule, not just raw signal incidence, before a fire-rate estimate can be trusted.

D1 SHOCK-FADE = LEAD NOT CANDIDATE (2026-07-23): the measured -3/-4% post-shock drift becomes a tradeable +3.68%/tr fade ONLY at a matched exit (72h pure hold; tp8/sl5 killed it via exit-shape mismatch) and ONLY at n=18 (fails n<30 quarantine), horizon-unstable (dead at 120h), riding a regime-dependent 'fade any large bar' base effect (placebo +1.3%, flips sign by regime). Real mechanism (shock beats placebo +2.4% at 72h) but sub-power and regime-bound. Pursue via SHADOW-ARM forward accumulation + cross-market equity power test + canary regime-gating; do NOT promote on n=18. Reaffirms: EXIT SHAPE MUST MATCH THESIS (fixing it revived a signal a mismatched exit had 'killed').

ideas.name: bollinger_family
ideas.result: all negative net; best n>100 variant -$0.03/trade gross before fees ate it
ideas.epitaph: Mean reversion at 5-min crypto scale exists but is smaller than fees. Also structurally mismatched to asymmetric +5/-3 exits, which want follow-through, not fades.

ideas.name: macross_family
ideas.result: all negative or zero-fire; slow variants fired 0-4 times in 6 months
ideas.epitaph: Crossovers either fire constantly into chop (fee bleed) or so rarely there is no sample. No parameterization escaped the dilemma.

ideas.name: liquidation_cascade_reclaim
ideas.epitaph: 4-sigma cascades with confirmed reclaims are so rare the filters starve the sample; what fired lost. The reclaim confirmation may arrive after the snap-back is spent.

ideas.name: btc_leads_alt_lag_capture
ideas.epitaph: The residual 5-min lead-lag is either gone or the freshness filters are stricter than the phenomenon. n=3 in 6 months is an idea that no longer occurs.

ideas.name: dead_zone_range_break
ideas.epitaph: Overnight coils exist and break, but breaks resolve under the fee floor as often as over it. The compression-release ratio didn't clear costs.

ideas.name: volume_thrust_regime_shift
ideas.tested: 2026-07-16 · 180d constrained replay (gauntlet) · LIVE A/B arm since 2026-07-16
ideas.result: backtest n=38, -$24.10, edge/slot -$0.63, win 36.8%
ideas.note: Live as an A/B arm against null, expected negative; promotion bar is beating null at p<0.05 over 100+ closed trades.

ideas.name: weekend_illiquidity_momentum
ideas.tested: 2026-07-16 · 180d constrained replay (gauntlet) · LIVE A/B arm since 2026-07-16
ideas.result: backtest n=79, -$16.22, edge/slot -$0.21, win 49.4%, GROSS-POSITIVE
ideas.note: The gross-positive/fee-negative exemplar. Live to test whether live behavior matches backtest.

ideas.name: null_baseline
ideas.result: permanent control arm

ideas.name: entropy_collapse_impulse
ideas.epitaph: Killed by own criterion (stop-rate >> target-rate). CALIBRATION: fired ~3x the predicted rate (3,283 candidates vs ~1,160 predicted) — the 15th-percentile coil is far more common than theorized, meaning low-entropy states are the norm, not a rare coiled spring. Entropy breaks are noise breaks at this bar scale.

ideas.name: omori_aftershock_ladder
ideas.epitaph: Killed by own criterion (net expectancy -0.47% < +0.3%). CALIBRATION: over-fired 2.6x predicted — 4-sigma 'mainshocks' are commoner than seismic intuition suggests (fat tails). The aftershock direction-persistence exists weakly (39.6% vs the asymmetric exit needing ~40%+) but is smaller than fees. Nearest-miss of the shock-family ideas.

ideas.name: failed_auction_rejection_wick
ideas.epitaph: Killed by own criterion (net expectancy -0.58% < +0.4%). Wick geometry at fresh extremes does not identify trapped flow — rejections were followed by continuation of the original push as often as reversal, exactly the failure the spec pre-named. Fade-shaped entries keep dying against the +5/-3 asymmetric exit; this is now a LESSON, not just an epitaph.

ideas.name: round_number_overshoot_snap
ideas.epitaph: Killed by own criterion. CALIBRATION: under-fired 5-8x predicted (202 candidates vs ~1,600) — clean overshoot-and-reject at FRESH round levels is rare, and what fired reverted less than fees. Another fade-shaped death.

ideas.name: drawdown_regime_contrarian_gate
ideas.result: n=460, -$301, edge/slot -$0.65, win 43.3%, gross Sharpe 0.98
ideas.epitaph: Killed by own criterion (armed expectancy -0.33% < +0.3%) — but the BEST of round 001 and the best win rate of any non-placebo idea ever tested. The self-referential gate carries real information (gross-positive shape, 43.3% wins against an asymmetric exit); the base breakout signal underneath it is too weak. LESSON for r002: the gate survived, the engine died — try better engines behind the same meta-gate.

ideas.name: conditional_entropy_regime_expansion
ideas.tested: 2026-07-17 · 930d constrained replay, taker AND maker fills
ideas.result: n=0 — NEVER FIRED in 2.5 years across 5 symbols
ideas.epitaph: The premise does not exist in the data: 5-min crypto sign sequences already run at H≈0.95 bits against a 1.0-bit ceiling (measured: mean order-2 conditional entropy 0.949, p99 jump +0.02, max ever +0.04 vs the required +0.35). There is no 'predictable regime' to break — the market lives at maximum entropy at this timescale, so an entropy SURGE is mathematically near-impossible, exactly as an entropy COLLAPSE (r001) was commonplace. The entropy lens is now dead in BOTH directions at 5-min resolution; if information theory returns, it must use magnitudes or longer bars, not signs.

ideas.name: epidemic_r0_crossover_ignition
ideas.epitaph: The growth-phase import died like the decay-phase import (Omori): R0>1.5 ignitions of signed volume ARE real events, but they mark the move's midpoint, not its beginning — by the time flow visibly self-amplifies at 5-min resolution the entry is chasing, and the -3% stop harvests the retraces. Continuation entries triggered by volume acceleration have now failed twice (vol_thrust marginal, r0 clearly negative). Even halved costs (maker) recover only a third of the loss: the timing is wrong, not the fees.

ideas.name: absorption_shelf_breakout
ideas.result: n=0 — NEVER FIRED; conjunction empty (of 19,788 windows: 442 pass 6-bar volume, 13 also pass small-body, 0 also pass tight-span)
ideas.epitaph: Absorption is invisible in OHLCV: volume and bar range are strongly POSITIVELY correlated in crypto, so 'high volume + small body' is anti-correlated and demanding it 6 bars running with tight overlap yields the empty set — measured funnel 19788 → 442 → 13 → 0. The spec's base-rate arithmetic multiplied the conjuncts as if independent; they are nearly mutually exclusive. True absorption needs order-book/footprint data (a Layer-2 argument), not candles.

ideas.name: options_expiry_pin_release
ideas.epitaph: The 08:00 UTC release window exists but carries no directional energy on 5-min spot bars: post-settlement breaks of a pre-08:00 pin resolved no better than any coil break (the dead_zone death, relocated to a named timestamp). Fire rate also missed 4x (predicted 0.15/sym/day, actual 0.04) — pins tight enough to qualify are rarer than the spec's arithmetic assumed, and the survivors were ordinary breakouts. Calendar structure alone doesn't make a coil directional; third coil-release death (dead_zone, weekend partial, this).

ideas.name: rejection_streak_gated_ignition
ideas.result: 930d: taker n=109, -$164, -$1.51/slot, win 36.7% (maker -$120, -$1.10/slot). BUT trailing 180d: +$15.24 taker (+0.22%/trade), +$26.30 maker (+0.38%/trade), n=35 — the ONLY stage-1-positive idea of rounds 001-002
ideas.epitaph: Dead by its own criterion over the full window (armed expectancy -0.75% < +0.3%): the saturation gate did not fix the R0 engine's chasing problem across 2.5 years. But the gradient is the loudest yet: positive on the trailing 180d under BOTH fill models, and the gate family is now best-of-round for the SECOND consecutive round (r001 regime gate 43.3% win, r002 gated engine only stage-1 survivor). Either the gate genuinely fits the current regime (testable: it goes on the watchlist for the NEXT 180d, out-of-sample) or best-of-round is what overfitting-to-recency looks like. Small n=35 forbids a claim; the next window will make one.

ideas.name: gap_fill_exhaustion_continuation
ideas.epitaph: 5-min crypto barely gaps: the full conjunction (>0.4% gap + held close + body>median + vol>median) fired 14 times in 2.5 YEARS across 5 symbols — spec predicted 8-15/sym/DAY (~2000x miss), and even the pre-implementation premise check (0.1/day for the raw gap alone) overestimated the conjunction 25x. The nearest-to-flat idea ever tested, so the mechanism is starved rather than refuted — but an engine firing 5x/year cannot feed a 5-slot book regardless of sign. If gaps return, lower the threshold and re-derive the base rate from the data first.

ideas.name: asian_to_london_handoff_thrust
ideas.epitaph: Crypto has no London open at 5-min scale: the Asian-range break during 07:00-09:00 UTC resolved like any other coil break — mostly failed. Fourth named-time coil death (dead_zone overnight, options_expiry 08:00, weekend, this): the clock does not lend a range break directional energy. Fire rate also missed ~50x (one qualifying break per 2.5 weeks, not 0.4/day).

ideas.name: post_shock_multiday_drift
ideas.result: n=35, -$72.49, edge/slot -$2.07, win 28.6%, net -1.04%/trade (kill bar was +0.5%)
ideas.epitaph: There is no multi-day drift after a 3% shock bar here: 71% of shocks hit the -5% stop before the +12% target across 120h — shock direction carries no 5-day signal. Confirms the r002 R0 lesson at a longer horizon: entering ON the visible impulse is chasing, and the horizon lever cannot rescue an entry with no directional edge — HORIZON IS A LEVER only multiplies an edge that exists. CORRECTION (2026-07-19): the control arm overturns the mechanism claim — shock direction DOES carry a multi-day signal, and it is CONTRARIAN (-3 to -4% sign-aligned). The strategy died by trading a real drift backwards, not by the drift's absence. The fade side is the largest gross effect yet measured here and is admissible for a proper spec.
ideas.control_arm: MEASURED 2026-07-19 (fragility re-analysis #2): sign-aligned forward returns after ALL 3%/3x-vol shocks, no barriers: 24h -2.97%+-1.00, 72h -4.32%+-1.31, 120h -3.33%+-1.50 (n=53, cascade clustering understates SEs).

ideas.name: pullback_to_breakout_level_limit
ideas.result: n=178, -$419.04, edge/slot -$2.35, win 30.9%, Sharpe -4.37, net -1.18%/trade — best-powered sample of the round
ideas.epitaph: The retest is not a discount — it is the failure signal. Breakouts that return to the broken level within 2h are disproportionately the ones that are FAILING; the runners you wanted never look back, so waiting at the level systematically selects the losers (win 30.9%, the worst Sharpe ever recorded here). Cost engineering cannot fix adverse selection in the entry: maker fills would recover ~0.2%/trade against a -1.18% expectancy. COST IS A DESIGN INPUT does not mean price-comes-to-you entries are free edge. CONFIRMED (2026-07-19): the control arm was measured and the adverse-selection mechanism holds at ~5 SE.
ideas.control_arm: MEASURED 2026-07-19 (fragility re-analysis #3): raw 24h forward returns, retested-within-2h breakouts -0.89%+-0.25 (n=197) vs never-retested +0.98%+-0.28 (n=128) — ~1.9% gap at ~5 SE. Adverse selection CONFIRMED on both arms.

ideas.name: slot_scarcity_conviction_gate
ideas.result: n=7, +$24.45, edge/slot +$3.49, win 71.4%, net +1.75%/trade — PASSED its kill bar (+0.3%) and beat the ungated engine (-0.16%)
ideas.epitaph: The first idea ever to pass its own kill criterion over the full window — and n=7 forbids believing it (the small-sample trap rule: n<30 means no claim regardless of sign). The gate opened rarely AND its gap engine fires 5x/year, so 2.5 years produced 7 armed trades. The gate family is now 3-for-3 best-of-round on three different engines (r001 regime gate 43% wins, r002 rejection gate stage-1 positive, r003 this) — the self-referential gate concentrates whatever edge exists, and every round the engine under it starves. PRESCRIPTION for the next gate idea: pair the gate with an engine firing >=1/sym/day so the armed sample reaches n>=100 per 930d and the gate's value becomes decidable.

ideas.name: trend_persistence_regime_gated_engine
ideas.result: n=39 armed/placed (74 raw armed-engine candidates), -$96.82, edge/slot -$2.48, win 25.6%, Sharpe -0.73
ideas.epitaph: The raw momentum-persistence engine comfortably cleared the r001-r003 prescription (>=1/sym/day: premise check measured 5.17/sym/day raw), but the ARMED sample still collapsed to n=39 over 930 days (0.008/sym/day, ~27x under the predicted 0.15-0.30 armed rate) — short of the idea's own n>=100 bar for a decisive family-retirement verdict. What did fire produced the worst win rate of any gate-family idea yet (25.6%, vs r001's 43.3% and r002's 36.7%) and a net expectancy of -1.24%/trade, leaving no realistic path to profitability regardless of the sample shortfall. Four consecutive rounds have now paired the self-referential gate with four different engines (r001 n=460, r002 n=109/35, r003 n=7, r004 n=39) and never once reached a statistically decidable armed sample. The r001-r003 prescription — 'pair the gate with a better engine' — is refuted as a fix: the bottleneck was never engine scarcity, it is the gate's own conjunctive definition (multiple system_state conditions required simultaneously) being intrinsically rare.

ideas.name: return_magnitude_compressibility_break
ideas.epitaph: The required pre-implementation premise check correctly confirmed magnitude headroom exists (p99 surprise 4.6, comfortably above the 3.0 threshold) — vindicating 'if information theory returns, use magnitudes not signs' as far as data existence goes. But headroom existing is not the same as the resulting novel-magnitude bar carrying directional continuation edge: the sign of a bar that breaks the recent quiet-magnitude code predicts the following bars no better than the fee floor allows (36.4% win against an exit needing ~40%+). Fired ~7x the predicted rate (0.73/sym/day vs 0.05-0.15) — quiet-then-surprise transitions are far more common in 5-min crypto than the anti-correlation assumption implied. Compressibility break identifies volatility-regime birth; it does not identify which direction that regime resolves.

ideas.name: hawkes_self_excitation_intensity_entry
ideas.epitaph: The idea's central premise — that 'high intensity AND low cumulative displacement' is a rare, anti-correlated conjunction marking a branching birth before a move — was wrong by two orders of magnitude: the premise check found the raw condition true ~29.7/sym/day (200-600x the predicted 0.05-0.15), and the full gauntlet confirmed the same scale (21.4/sym/day). The 'loading phase' the Hawkes import expected to be a brief, informative window is instead close to the market's default state at 5-min resolution, with no bearing on subsequent displacement (37.9% win, no better than entering on the visible impulse). This closes the shock/point-process import family 3-for-3 dead: Omori's decay-phase entry (r001) chased a spent cluster, R0's growth-phase crossing (r002) marked the midpoint, and Hawkes's pre-displacement loading phase (r004) turns out not to be a distinct, tradable regime at all. Timing the entry earlier in the self-excitation lifecycle does not rescue the family.

ideas.name: one_sided_range_expansion_thrust
ideas.epitaph: The engineered inversion worked exactly as designed on the base-rate front — large-body-plus-volume is positively correlated as predicted, the conjunction was populated (not the absorption empty set), and fire rate landed close to the predicted 0.5-1.5/sym/day band (0.68 actual). But populated does not mean informative: a clean one-sided thrust (tiny opposing wick, close pinned at a fresh extreme, elevated volume) continues no better than a random large bar — worse, in fact, than the idea's own named decisive-kill comparison (34.4% win, below the ~38% breakeven reference, and the worst Sharpe of any candidate this round). This is the fourth consecutive microstructure_from_ohlcv death and the best-sampled one yet (wick rejection r001, absorption r002 empty-set, gap-fill r003 starved, this one well-powered and still negative): every OHLCV candle-geometry shape tried — fade, absorption, gap, one-sided expansion — has failed. The lens needs order-book/footprint data to proceed; candle geometry alone is exhausted.

ideas.name: round_number_breach_continuation
ideas.result: n=601, -$447.03, edge/slot -$0.74, win 42.3%, Sharpe 1.67 (gross-positive shape)
ideas.epitaph: The best win rate (42.3%) and only positive Sharpe of the round — another instance of the GROSS-POSITIVE trap shape, not a live edge. The stop-cascade-fuel thesis (a decisive breach of a coiled round-number level recruits resting orders on the far side) is directionally plausible and shows up in the numbers, but the recruited energy is smaller than the fee floor demands (-0.37%/trade net). Fired ~10x the predicted rate (1.08/sym/day vs 0.06-0.15) — coiling near a round level is common, not the rare magnet event assumed. With this death, the round-number price level has now failed in BOTH directions (r001 fade, r004 continuation), closing out the behavioral_calendar lens 4-for-4 dead.

ideas.name: placebo_losing_streak_single_gate_trend
ideas.tested: 2026-07-20 · 930d constrained replay, taker AND maker fills
ideas.result: n=3006 taker/3005 maker, -$3,368.90 taker/-$2,529.02 maker, edge/slot -$1.12 taker/-$0.84 maker, win 38.7% taker/39.7% maker, Sharpe -0.66/-0.44
ideas.epitaph: Premortem verdict: SKIP — it predicted this idea re-tests r001's already-falsified gate-plus-weak-breakout-engine shape, and that the maker-fee claim contradicts a chase-style (price-closes-beyond-prior-bar) entry. Both predictions held. The single-scalar-gate fix (one trailing placebo-hit-rate threshold instead of r004's multi-condition system_state conjunction) worked exactly as designed on sample size: armed n=3006/3005, the best-sampled gate-family idea ever tested, no starvation at all. But the engine underneath is the same species r001 already falsified at n=460 (43.3% win, -0.33%/trade net) — a with-trend continuation confirmed by a bar-close breakout — and here it does WORSE with the identical gate family (38.7-39.7% win, -0.42% to -0.56%/trade net). Loosening the gate to a single scalar does not fix a weak engine; the gate was never the bottleneck for this engine shape, and r001 already proved it.
ideas.control_arm: r001 drawdown_regime_contrarian_gate (dead-ideas.json): same self-referential gate family, weak intraday breakout engine, n=460, 43.3% win, gross Sharpe 0.98, -0.33%/trade net — this idea pairs a looser gate with the same engine species and scores worse.

ideas.name: trailing_return_rank_persistence_hold
ideas.tested: 2026-07-20 · 930d constrained replay, taker AND maker fills
ideas.result: n=536 taker/589 maker, -$1,665.92 taker/-$1,710.48 maker, edge/slot -$3.11 taker/-$2.90 maker, win 29.5% taker/29.5% maker, Sharpe -1.99/-2.11
ideas.epitaph: Premortem verdict: IMPLEMENT — found no fatal flaw and called the fire-rate margin comfortable (n>=465 even at half the estimate). That margin held (n=536/589), but the published cross-sectional-momentum-persistence premise it rested on does not hold in THIS basket: win rate (29.5% both fill models) sits below the idea's own 33% kill bar and even below the raw +15/-6 breakeven of ~29%, net expectancy -1.45% to -1.55%/trade — the worst of the round. Being the top-ranked-by-7-day-return symbol of 5 predicts continuation no better than chance weighted toward reversion. This lands inside the epoch this project's own regime-split grid already flagged: directional momentum in this exact 2024-25 archive runs negative, so a 5-asset basket's cross-sectional 'leadership' is just single-asset momentum wearing a ranking costume, and momentum is the one thing measured to have flipped sign here.
ideas.control_arm: REGIME IS THE VARIABLE (dead-ideas.json failure_lessons): directional monthly momentum earned +13.4% to +28.8%/hold in 2020-23, then flipped to -12.4% (0/9 cells) in 2024-25 — the epoch this idea's leadership signal was tested against.

ideas.name: weekly_pullback_limit_into_uptrend
ideas.tested: 2026-07-20 · 930d constrained replay, taker AND maker fills
ideas.result: n=3 taker/3 maker, +$34.59 taker/+$35.86 maker, edge/slot +$11.53 taker/+$11.95 maker, win 66.7%/66.7%, Sharpe 0.69/0.70
ideas.epitaph: Premortem verdict: IMPLEMENT — found no fatal flaw, correctly named the residual risk (does requiring a touchable pullback select the weaker trends inside an uptrend bucket, echoing r003's retest-selects-failure mechanism) as 'the hypothesis under test,' and recommended implementing to get a decisive answer rather than skip pre-emptively. No decisive answer arrived: the idea starved to n=3 over 930 days, ~186x under its own base-rate estimate (0.12/sym-day predicted vs 0.0006 actual) — the worst fire-rate miss on record, past slot_scarcity_conviction_gate's 166x. The 3 trades that fired look spectacular (66.7% win, +5.8-6.0%/trade net) but the gauntlet's own small-sample-trap warning applies verbatim: n<30 means any claim is statistically unreliable regardless of sign. 'Weekly uptrend >8% AND a touchable 3%-below-24h-high pullback within 12h' is almost never jointly satisfied in this basket. This gets no claim, positive or negative, and does not qualify for holdout or live-arm consideration at this n.
ideas.control_arm: r003 pullback_to_breakout_level_limit (dead-ideas.json): intraday analog of the same retest-selects-failure mechanism, well-powered n, 30.9% win, worst Sharpe on record — the premortem's flagged risk, unresolved here at n=3.

ideas.name: placebo_streak_gated_weekly_trend_engine
ideas.tested: 2026-07-20 · 930d constrained replay, taker AND maker fills
ideas.result: n=407 taker/447 maker, -$1,226.48 taker/-$1,341.98 maker, edge/slot -$3.01 taker/-$3.00 maker, win 30.7% taker/30.0% maker, Sharpe -1.66/-1.93
ideas.epitaph: Premortem verdict: REDESIGN — warned the stated 2.5x sample cushion was thinner than any historical miss for the self-referential-gate family (3x-166x) and recommended validating the gate's real open-rate before spending the gauntlet. That specific risk did NOT materialize: the gate fired MORE than predicted (0.0876-0.0961/sym-day actual vs 0.054 predicted, ~1.6-1.8x over, not under) and the sample landed at n=407/447, the second-largest gate-family sample ever after r001's n=460 — comfortably decidable. But the idea's own pre-registered kill condition fired anyway: armed win rate (30.7%/30.0%) is statistically indistinguishable from this SAME ROUND's ungated version of the identical weekly-trend engine (trailing_return_rank_persistence_hold: 29.5%/29.5%, net -1.55%/-1.45% vs gated -1.51%/-1.50%) — the gate added no lift whatsoever. This is the fifth and most decisively-powered self-referential-gate death; per its own pre-registered stop ('if this ... still cannot beat its ungated engine at n>=100, the entire gate family is retired permanently'), the family is now closed for good, confirming at large n what the 2026-07-19 hierarchical meta-analysis freeze already found at pooled-small-n: the gate carries no measurable information over an engine's unconditioned trade stream.
ideas.control_arm: same-round ungated engine trailing_return_rank_persistence_hold: n=536 taker/589 maker, win 29.5%/29.5%, net -1.55%/-1.45%/trade — statistically indistinguishable from this idea's gated 30.7%/30.0%, -1.51%/-1.50%.

ideas.name: multiday_magnitude_persistence_directional_hold
ideas.tested: 2026-07-20 · 930d constrained replay, taker AND maker fills
ideas.result: n=286 taker/305 maker, -$586.26 taker/-$389.73 maker, edge/slot -$2.05 taker/-$1.28 maker, win 31.5% taker/34.1% maker, Sharpe -0.80/-0.39
ideas.epitaph: Premortem verdict: IMPLEMENT — called this 'the most self-aware spec of the round,' credited it for using magnitude-persistence only as a regime-quality gate and taking direction from trend sign, never asking entropy/magnitude for a sign (the literal r004 prescription), and found no fatal flaw. It died anyway: win rate is below the idea's own 33% bar on taker fills (31.5%) and net expectancy is negative on both fill models (-1.02%/-0.64%, both under the +0.5% kill line). The direction-from-trend half of the design inherits the same problem killing this round's other trend-direction idea: a genuine vol-clustering regime does not, in this basket and epoch, mean the co-occurring 7-day trend is more likely to continue — using entropy/magnitude correctly as a regime-quality filter (not a direction signal, as prescribed) does not rescue a directional bet made in a reversion epoch. Fire-rate arithmetic also over-shot: predicted ~0.24/sym-day (positive-correlation-adjusted), actual 0.0615-0.0656 (~3.7-3.9x under) — the argued positive correlation between magnitude-persistence and trend-strength was weaker than assumed.
ideas.control_arm: REGIME IS THE VARIABLE (dead-ideas.json failure_lessons): directional monthly momentum flipped from +13.4%-+28.8%/hold (2020-23) to -12.4% (2024-25, 0/9 cells) — the same epoch this idea's trend-direction component was tested against, and the same mechanism killing this round's trailing_return_rank_persistence_hold.

ideas.name: predator_prey_volume_depletion_rebound
ideas.tested: 2026-07-22 · 930d constrained replay, taker AND maker fills
ideas.result: n=62 taker/58 maker, -$77.49 taker/-$31.72 maker, edge/slot -$1.25 taker/-$0.55 maker, win 35.5%/39.7%, Sharpe -0.12/0.10
ideas.epitaph: Premortem verdict: REDESIGN — flagged that the spec's claimed 0.25-0.35 conditional probability of volume-decline-given-3-up-days contradicted its own 'lower than baseline' argument (naive baseline 1/6≈0.167) and demanded a direct histogram before spending a slot. The check was run and vindicated the premortem almost exactly: measured P(vol monotonic decline | 3 up-days) = 0.162 (84/519) — the naive baseline, not the spec's inflated 0.25-0.35. Implemented unchanged per spec (letting the gauntlet measure the true rate rather than hand-tuning after a miss). Full-conjunction fire count landed at n=64 project-wide (62 taker/58 maker after fill-model splits), narrowly below the idea's own n>=100 decidability floor — the premortem's secondary inconclusive-risk also materialized, just mildly rather than catastrophically. On the sample gauntled, win rate (35.5%/39.7%) cleared the nominal 33% breakeven for +10%/-5% exits, but only 10 of 62 taker trades (16%) actually resolved at take_profit; 30 (48%) timed out at diluted magnitudes far short of the 10% target, and 22 (35%) hit the full stop — so net expectancy stayed negative-to-negligible (-0.13%/+0.11%/trade, both under the +0.5% kill bar) despite the win-rate arithmetic looking survivable. Win rate above nominal tp/sl breakeven does not imply positive expectancy when time-exit, not tp/sl, is the modal outcome — the realized payout ratio is much flatter than the stated exits imply.

ideas.name: range_compression_then_directional_expansion_gap
ideas.result: n=84 taker/84 maker, -$23.54 taker/-$15.78 maker, edge/slot -$0.28 taker/-$0.19 maker, win 47.6%/47.6%, Sharpe 1.67/1.38
ideas.epitaph: Premortem verdict: SKIP — the two-phase TR-compression/expansion framing is still pure OHLCV candle geometry, the identical data source behind the microstructure lens's 4-for-4 death record, plus an independent ceiling conflict (same-bar resolution sits in the measured-empty sub-12h band). Implemented anyway per round policy. Confirmed dead: killed at the 180d stage on both fill models. The gauntlet surfaces a subtlety the premortem didn't need to resolve: mean per-trade return was actually POSITIVE (+0.36% taker/+0.29% maker, win rate 47.6% both) — the shape looks survivable read as percentages — yet dollar-weighted edge/slot, this project's actual fitness metric, was negative both ways (-$0.28/-$0.19). The reason is sizing, not signal: stop-loss exits carry consistently larger notional (~$233 in the sampled trades) than the smaller, more variable time-exit wins, so the dollar P&L is worse than the naive %-average implies. A fifth confirmation that OHLCV candle geometry alone is exhausted — the lens needs order-book/footprint data to proceed, and no repackaging of the same four inputs (wick, body, volume, sequencing) has escaped that ceiling in five tries.
ideas.control_arm: MICROSTRUCTURE-FROM-OHLCV LENS EXHAUSTED (dead-ideas.json failure_lessons): wick rejection, absorption, gap-fill, one-sided thrust — 4/4 prior candle-geometry shapes dead, same OHLCV-only data source this idea repackages as a two-phase TR compression/expansion sequence.

ideas.name: month_end_rebalance_flow_directional_persistence
ideas.tested: 2026-07-22 · 930d constrained replay, taker AND maker fills
ideas.result: n=105 taker/123 maker, -$177.64 taker/-$274.04 maker, edge/slot -$1.69 taker/-$2.23 maker, win 32.4%/30.1%, Sharpe -0.43/-1.03
ideas.epitaph: Premortem verdict: SKIP — the rebalance-flow narrative supplies no new data source (UTC month-boundary timestamp + prior-month OHLCV return, the same two ingredients behind four dead calendar/price-level ideas), bets on monthly-return continuation in the exact epoch REGIME IS THE VARIABLE measured at -12.4%/0-9, and hedges its own directional sign post hoc ('test long-only if short arm degenerate') rather than committing to one. Implemented with the clean symmetric sign rule the spec itself states, deliberately NOT carrying the post-hoc hedge into code, so the gauntlet saw both arms honestly. All three premortem objections confirmed: dead on both fill models, win rate (32.4%/30.1%) at or below the idea's own ~33% breakeven, edge/slot solidly negative (-$1.69/-$2.23), and the fire-rate premise check (73-80% of symbol-months qualify, vs. the spec's assumed ~70%) was the most accurate base-rate prediction in the round — this death is not a decidability problem, it is the regime problem, confirmed with a comfortable sample.
ideas.control_arm: BEHAVIORAL-CALENDAR LENS EXHAUSTED (failure_lessons): round-number fade/continuation and named clock-time coils 4-for-4 dead, no data source beyond OHLCV timestamp+price; REGIME IS THE VARIABLE (failure_lessons): directional monthly momentum measured at -12.4% (0/9 cells) across this project's entire 2024-25 archive — the exact continuation bet this idea's month-start entry makes.

ideas.name: constraint_rejection_pressure_release_engine
ideas.result: n=556 taker/559 maker, -$728.00 taker/-$578.16 maker, edge/slot -$1.31 taker/-$1.03 maker, win 34.7%/35.8%, Sharpe -1.50/-1.32, max_dd 73.2%/59.0%
ideas.epitaph: Premortem verdict: REDESIGN — accepted the gate-family reopening as legitimate (a cross-sectional synchronization count is genuinely absent from any single engine's own trade stream, unlike the five closed-family specs that all conditioned on their own past outcomes), but warned the entry structure (this symbol's breakout is the CURRENT bar, nested inside a S>=3 synchronization window) matched the exact shape of the two worst historical fire-rate misses on record (166x, 186x under-prediction) and demanded a premise check before trusting the arithmetic. The premise check was run (22,118 of 31,892 raw breakout events, 69.3%, co-occurred with S>=3 — far ABOVE the spec's assumed 25-40%) and it caught a real miscalibration, but in the OPPOSITE direction the premortem feared: this idea over-fired, not under-fired, because crypto breakouts are more cross-sectionally synchronized than even the spec's own correlation-adjusted estimate assumed. Killed at the 180d stage on both fill models anyway — win rate (34.7%/35.8%) sits below the idea's own 38% breakeven, net expectancy is deeply negative (-0.16%/trade taker), and max drawdown (73%/59%) is the worst of the round by a wide margin. The cross-sectional-synchronization MECHANISM itself remains formally untested against its intended control (this symbol's own ungated breakouts) — that comparison was not run this round, so the family-reopening question is undecided on its own terms even though this specific spec is unambiguously dead on absolute performance.

ideas.name: multiweek_directional_regime_persistence_hold
ideas.result: n=50 taker/48 maker, -$324.55 taker/-$323.96 maker, edge/slot -$6.49 taker/-$6.75 maker, win 30.0%/29.2%, Sharpe -5.23/-5.62, max_dd 32.9%/33.0%
ideas.epitaph: Premortem verdict: IMPLEMENT — the round's mandated continuation canary (CANARY MANDATE), found no fatal paper-detectable flaw, credited it for being the least fragile base-rate math in the round (a single univariate |R14|>6% threshold, not a multi-condition or nested-timing conjunction) and for correctly occupying the one band REGIME IS THE VARIABLE / REVERSION AT EVERY MEASURED HORIZON left explicitly OPEN (2-week TSMOM, where directional momentum previously beat drift). The base-rate math held up as promised: per-symbol qualification measured at 47-71% pre-implementation (spec assumed 55-65%), and the realized fire rate (0.056/sym/day taker, pro-rated from the 180d stage) lands within the calibration scatter this project already expects. But the canary is designed to be informative either way, and it fired NEGATIVE, decisively: win rate 30.0%/29.2% (nowhere near the ~40% breakeven for +15%/-7% exits), edge/slot -$6.49/-$6.75 — the worst in the round — and Sharpe -5.2/-5.6, killed at the 180d stage on both fill models before the full 930d run. Per its own kill criterion ('a sign-flip in its result IS the regime-flip alarm — a positive result is itself the load-bearing signal, not just a pass'), this result is the alarm NOT sounding: the 2-week-to-7-day band, the one directional-continuation territory this project's regime-split grid had not yet tested, now also fails — the reversion regime that governs 1-6mo horizons extends down into the 2-week band too. The premortem's paper review was correct that there was no flaw to find; the flaw was empirical, in the regime, and only the gauntlet could see it — exactly the blind spot PREMORTEM CANNOT SEE REGIME RISK (new lesson, promoted this round after its second occurrence) now names.
ideas.control_arm: REVERSION AT EVERY MEASURED HORIZON (failure_lessons): long-or-flat momentum underperformed drift in 9/9 cells of the {1,3,6}mo grid; REGIME IS THE VARIABLE (failure_lessons): directional monthly momentum flipped to -12.4% (0/9 cells) across the entire 2024-25 archive — this idea's 2-week/7-day band was the one open question those lessons left standing, and it is now closed in the same direction.

ideas.name: liquidity_window_shock_fade_maker
ideas.tested: 2026-07-23 · 930d constrained replay, taker AND maker fills
ideas.result: n=41 taker/41 maker, -$28.11 taker/-$54.97 maker, edge/slot -$0.69 taker/-$1.34 maker, win 41.5%/39.0%, Sharpe 0.31/-0.58
ideas.epitaph: Premortem verdict: REDESIGN — flagged an internal contradiction (params stayed at the strict 0.03/3.0 shock trigger while the fire-rate math assumed a loosened 0.02/2.5 trigger), predicted that as originally coded the real population was ~53 events yielding only ~9-10 placed trades, squarely inconclusive_starved, and separately warned the extension-to-fill maker condition is a rare-touch-conjunction-inside-a-rare-event, the shape that missed base rates by 166x-186x twice before. The prescribed fix (shock_pct 0.03→0.02, vol_mult 3.0→2.5) was applied exactly as asked, and the premise check confirmed it worked on paper: the loosened trigger measures 728 raw shocks with a 66% extension-fill rate (480 filled), an order of magnitude above the spec's own conservative assumption. But that raw population never reached the gauntlet — CONSTRAINT DOMINANCE (5 slots, $1k ceiling, 1h cooldown, and here a 96h hold that occupies a symbol's slot for days per fill) crushed 480 premise-checked fills down to 41 candidates placed, still under the idea's own n>=100 decidability bar. Net expectancy landed below the +0.5%/trade kill bar in both fill models regardless (+0.16% taker, -0.30% maker) with win rate 39-41%, so the question is moot: the redesign fixed exactly the bug premortem found, and the strategy still died, both underpowered and unprofitable.
ideas.control_arm: SHOCKS MEAN-REVERT AT MULTI-DAY HORIZON (2026-07-19, n=53 unconditional shock population, -3.0%/-4.3%/-3.3% at 24/72/120h) is the reversion baseline this maker order tried to harvest with a better entry price than a chasing taker fill — the strategy could not convert that measured population edge into a profitable, adequately-sampled book once portfolio constraints were applied.

ideas.name: cross_sectional_dispersion_gate_trend_engine
ideas.tested: 2026-07-23 · 930d constrained replay, taker AND maker fills
ideas.result: n=387 taker/392 maker, -$379.17 taker/-$301.75 maker, edge/slot -$0.98 taker/-$0.77 maker, win 40.1%/39.8%, Sharpe 0.08/-0.02
ideas.epitaph: Premortem verdict: IMPLEMENT — the round's mandated continuation canary, credited with a genuine gate reopening (cross-sectional basket dispersion is external information no single engine's own trade stream carries, the same reopening argument r006's constraint_rejection_pressure_release_engine used) and with closing that idea's stated gap by writing a kill_criterion requiring the gated engine to beat the SAME engine run ungated at n>=100. It landed comfortably decidable as predicted (n=387/392, vs. calibrated ~140) and died as a clean negative canary: continuation is still dead in the synchronized-dispersion subset, matching REGIME IS THE VARIABLE and REVERSION AT EVERY MEASURED HORIZON rather than overturning them — the canary did its job. But the promised control never ran: no ungated companion variant of this engine appears anywhere in this round's gauntlet, so the actual "does the gate carry information" question premortem said this spec would finally answer is, again, undecided on its own terms; net expectancy (+0.01%/-0.00%) was below the kill bar's fallback clause regardless, so the idea died without ever being tested against its own named control. This is the SECOND time in two rounds a gate-family kill_criterion has promised a same-engine ungated comparison that was never executed (r006 constraint_rejection_pressure_release_engine made the identical promise) — promoted to failure_lessons this round.

ideas.name: kolmogorov_directional_asymmetry_break
ideas.tested: 2026-07-23 · 930d constrained replay, taker AND maker fills
ideas.result: n=706 taker/702 maker, -$682.35 taker/-$356.19 maker, edge/slot -$0.97 taker/-$0.51 maker, win 37.8%/39.7%, Sharpe 0.08/0.54
ideas.epitaph: Premortem verdict: REDESIGN — caught that the spec as written took direction from trend sign, with the novel compressibility-asymmetry statistic acting only as a gate, exactly the shape of r005's multiday_magnitude_persistence_directional_hold ("info-theoretic regime gate + direction from trend sign"), which premortem itself called the most self-aware spec of that round and which died anyway (n=286/305, -$2.05/-$1.28/slot, win 31.5%/34.1%) because trend continuation is dead in this epoch regardless of gate quality. The prescribed fix (drop the `close > close[lookback]` clause, take direction purely from sign(A)) was applied, so this result genuinely tests something r005 never did: whether up/down compressibility asymmetry alone predicts direction. It does not — win rate (37.8%/39.7%) sits below the spec's own ~40% breakeven for its +6%/-3% exits, and the sample is not the problem (n=706/702, close to the calibration-doubled ~744 the <0.3/sym/day bucket predicts from the spec's own 372 estimate, comfortably decidable). The redesign succeeded in isolating a clean test of a genuinely new statistic, and that statistic carries no directional information on its own — a sharper result than r005 managed, not a repeat of it.
ideas.control_arm: multiday_magnitude_persistence_directional_hold (r005, n=286 taker/305 maker, -$2.05/-$1.28 edge/slot, win 31.5%/34.1%) — the nearest-ancestor gate+trend-sign shape this redesign was built to avoid reproducing; this result is a distinct, independently-negative test of the ungated statistic itself.

ideas.name: epidemic_susceptible_depletion_terminal_burst
ideas.tested: 2026-07-23 · 930d constrained replay, taker AND maker fills
ideas.result: n=42 taker/35 maker, -$18.26 taker/-$29.18 maker, edge/slot -$0.43 taker/-$0.83 maker, win 45.2%/42.9%, Sharpe 0.75/-0.10
ideas.epitaph: Premortem verdict: REDESIGN — flagged this as the second volume-exhaustion cross-domain idea in two rounds (after r006's predator_prey_volume_depletion_rebound, which landed at n=62/58 with a near-null edge, "no claim, positive or negative") and calculated the spec's own estimate calibrates to n≈84, below the n>=100 decidability floor, in the same near-miss band. The prescribed fix (raise the raw run-population rate) was applied via decline_bars 4→2 (the real lever, per the premise check: run_pct alone only reached n=5, while dropping decline_bars reached n=310 raw) — a hundred-fold increase in the raw conjunction population. It made no difference to the constrained outcome: CONSTRAINT DOMINANCE (5 slots, cooldown, 48h hold occupying the slot) crushed 310 raw premise-checked events down to 42/35 placed, an even smaller sample than premortem's original, unfixed-spec estimate of ~84 and squarely inside the predator_prey near-miss band it warned about. Two volume-exhaustion cross-domain ideas, two rounds, two starved near-misses — the efficiency-ratio framing (this idea) versus raw volume-decline (predator_prey) is a real mechanistic distinction that made no measurable difference to the outcome shape.
ideas.control_arm: predator_prey_volume_depletion_rebound (r006, n=62 taker/58 maker, -$1.25/-$0.55 edge/slot, win 35.5%/39.7%, "no claim") — the directly comparable prior volume-exhaustion measurement this idea explicitly set out to improve on and landed beside instead.

ideas.name: trapped_breakout_volume_void_reversal
ideas.tested: 2026-07-23 · 930d constrained replay, taker AND maker fills
ideas.result: n=289 taker/282 maker, -$466.14 taker/-$356.39 maker, edge/slot -$1.61 taker/-$1.26 maker, win 39.5%/40.4%, Sharpe -2.79/-2.15
ideas.epitaph: Premortem verdict: SKIP — MICROSTRUCTURE-FROM-OHLCV LENS EXHAUSTED, the lens's 5th confirmed death (wick rejection, absorption empty-set, gap-fill starved, one-sided range expansion, range-compression-then-expansion-gap), all built from the same four inputs (wick, body, volume, sequencing); premortem judged the "two-phase flow-order" framing here more elaborate than its five predecessors but not a fundamentally different data source, and separately calculated a starvation risk (n≈62) independent of the lens argument. The starvation call was wrong — the premise check found the low-volume-breakout/re-entry conjunction not anti-correlated (2.7% frac, 69% re-entry conditional, n=1056 raw), and the constrained gauntlet placed 289/282, comfortably decidable. The lens call was right: implemented anyway per round policy, this is now the 6th confirmed death of the same closed lens, with the worst Sharpe of the round (-2.79/-2.15) and a win rate (39.5%/40.4%) that never approached its own ~38% breakeven margin. A sixth distinct geometric permutation of wick+body+volume+sequencing has now failed on the same OHLCV data source; the lens needs order-book/footprint data to proceed, not another shape.
ideas.control_arm: one_sided_range_expansion_thrust (r004, n=465, -$1.43 edge/slot, win 34.4%, Sharpe -2.48) — the previous best-sampled entry in this lens, cited as the lens's baseline; this result extends the same closed-lens verdict with a comparably negative Sharpe.
